An old man named Harsk runs the closest thing this community has to a government. He sits by a barrel fire, feeding it scraps of plastic that burn with chemical-green flames. 'Haven't seen you in weeks,' he says. 'Thought maybe you'd finally phased yourself into oblivion.'
'Not yet,' you say. 'I need information. Division 9 is active in the tunnels. More active than usual.'
Harsk's face darkens. 'Tell me something I don't know. They took Old Maya last week. Walked right into her shelter and dragged her out. She wasn't even Shadowkin. Just in the wrong place.'

choices.text: "We need to warn people. Get organized."
"Have you heard of the Fold?"

shd_067.title: Surfacing with Pyx
text: Getting Pyx topside is harder than getting yourself there. The child can barely walk, the sedatives still dragging at their limbs, and every sudden noise triggers a partial phase that makes your grip on their hand slip like holding smoke.

You emerge from the maintenance hatch into the rain-soaked alley behind Bao's noodle stand. The old woman takes one look at the child, the too-big eyes, the flickering outline, the Division 9 restraint marks on their wrists, and her face goes stone-hard.

'Inside,' she says, pulling open the back door of the space behind her counter. It's a storage room barely bigger than a closet, stacked with supplies and smelling of broth and ginger. She clears a space, lays out a blanket, and guides Pyx down onto it with the gentle efficiency of someone who's cared for strays before.
'Division?' Bao asks, one word, her eyes on the restraint marks.
You nod.
She reaches under the counter and pulls out a comm unit. Old, beat-up, but functional. 'I know someone,' she says. 'Someone who helps people like this one. I'll make a call.'

choices.text: Wait for Bao's contact
"Thank you, Bao. I need to find help too."

shd_068.title: Pyx's Memory
text: Pyx's silver eyes go distant, the way eyes do when they're looking at memories instead of walls. 'They had a machine,' the child says. 'A big one. It filled a whole room. It made a sound like the inside of your head when you're about to phase, that buzzing, that pulling feeling. They called it the Resonance Engine.'

'They made me stand in front of it. Close. So close I could feel it pulling at me, like it was trying to turn me inside out. And when it was running, I could see... things. In the between-space. Shapes. Moving. Not random, moving with purpose. Like they were looking for something.'

Pyx shivers. 'The scientists were excited. They said the Engine was working. They said they were going to open a door. A permanent door. No more phasing in and out. A way to reach the between-space and stay there. Or a way to bring what's in there out here.'
'They said it would change everything. They said it would make Meridian the most powerful corporation in the world. They said it was worth any cost.'
Any cost. Including the lives of children who phase between dimensions.
The Resonance Engine. You need to tell someone about this. Someone who can stop it.

choices.text: Get Pyx somewhere safe, then find the Fold
Head straight for the sanctuary with Pyx

shd_069.title: Rest
text: You sleep in shifts. Or you try. The tunnel is cold and damp, the walls slick with condensation that catches the faint light of your lamp and turns it into something almost beautiful. Almost.

When you close your eyes, the between-space is there. Waiting. It's always there when you sleep, pressing against the membrane of your consciousness, whispering in frequencies that your brain translates as half-formed thoughts. Tonight it's louder. More insistent. As if something in the dark between dimensions knows you're close to a threshold you haven't crossed yet.

You dream of your mother. She's standing in a room you don't recognize, surrounded by equipment that hums with the phase frequency. She's looking at something you can't see, and her expression is the one she wore the night she told you to run. Not fear. Determination. The face of someone who's made a decision that will cost them everything.
You wake gasping, your outline rippling, your hands passing through the concrete floor up to the wrists. You pull yourself solid with a wrench of will that leaves you shaking and nauseous.
Morning. Or what passes for morning in a place without sunlight. Time to move.

choices.text: Head for the Fold's sanctuary
Go topside to Mira's shop

shd_070.title: The Truth
text: Lira leads you to the far side of the sanctuary, where the mural covers the wall. Up close, you can see the detail. The figures emerging from shadow aren't generic. They're specific. Faces you don't recognize, but Lira does. She touches the wall with her glass hand.

'These are the ones we've lost. Seventeen Shadowkin who phased and never came back. Some taken by Division 9. Some lost to the between. Some who couldn't take the isolation and the fear and chose to stop fighting for solidity.'

She turns to you. 'You were born from a mistake. Twenty-three years ago, Meridian conducted an experiment called Project Threshold. They were trying to open a stable gateway between our dimension and the between-space. They believed the between contained energy sources, resources, possibly even habitable space. They were wrong about the habitable part.'

'The experiment failed. The gateway collapsed. The dimensional backlash created a rift that spread through the Lower District like a shockwave, passing through pregnant women, through children, through anyone whose neural development was still plastic enough to be rewritten. That's what made us. Not evolution. Not mutation. An accident. A corporate mistake that Meridian has spent two decades trying to study, exploit, and cover up.'
'We are the rift's children. And the rift isn't done with us.'

choices.text: "What do you mean, not done with us?"
"What do we do now?"

shd_071.title: What's Coming
text: Lira's face is grave. 'The phases are accelerating. All of us, every Shadowkin in the Fold, is experiencing the same thing. Longer phases, deeper immersion in the between-space, and something new: synchronized events. Two days ago, every Shadowkin in the sanctuary phased simultaneously. For exactly eleven seconds, not one of us was solid. The same thing, at the same time.'
'Something in the between-space is resonating with us. Calling to us. And every time we phase, we answer, and the barrier gets a little thinner.'

She brings up a map projected from a device on her workbench. It shows the Lower District, overlaid with phase event data. Clusters of activity, concentrated around the Foundry district. 'The epicenter is here. The old lab. The place where Project Threshold originated. Whatever they started twenty-three years ago is still running, still active, still tearing at the dimensional barrier from both sides.'

'Division 9 knows. That's why they're accelerating their capture program. They're not just studying us anymore. They're building something. A device to channel our phase abilities, to use us as living keys to the between-space. They call it the Resonance Engine.'
'And we're running out of time to stop it.'

choices.text: "Then we move against Division 9. Now."
"What's the plan?"

shd_072.title: Targeted
text: Lira nods. 'Your phase signature is strong. Stronger than most. You've been living alone, phasing regularly, with nothing but a homemade stabilizer between you and dissolution. That takes willpower. Talent. Stubbornness. All things we need.'

'We've been monitoring the phase events in this sector for months. Your signature kept appearing, strong and stable, in a part of the tunnels where Division 9 hasn't established surveillance yet. That makes you both powerful and vulnerable. Powerful because you've survived on your own. Vulnerable because Division 9 will find you eventually. They always do.'

She gestures at the cavern around her. 'Here, you have support. Training. People who understand what you're going through because they're going through it too. And when Division 9 comes, because they will, you won't face them alone.'
The offer hangs in the air between you. Join or stay alone. Community or isolation. Safety in numbers or safety in solitude.
You've been alone for eight years. It's gotten you this far. But this far isn't far enough.

choices.text: "I'm in. What do you need from me?"
"Tell me what we're up against first."

shd_073.title: Commitment
text: The words come out with a ferocity that surprises you. 'I want to fight.' Because the alternative is what you've been doing for eight years: surviving. Hiding. Watching other Shadowkin get dragged away while you press yourself into walls and tell yourself it's not your problem.

It's your problem. It's always been your problem. Every child who phases for the first time and falls through a floor in terror, every mother who watches her child flicker and fade and doesn't know why, every Shadowkin who disappears into a Division 9 facility and never comes out. That's you. That's your kind. That's the family you never knew you had.

Lira studies your face. Whatever she sees there must satisfy her, because she nods, a single sharp motion that carries the weight of a decision made. 'Good. We need fighters. But more than that, we need people who can go where we can't. Your phase abilities are raw, but they're powerful. With training, you could be the best infiltrator we have.'
'An infiltrator who can walk through any wall, bypass any security, reach any target.'
She extends her glass hand. 'Welcome to the Fold.'

choices.text: Begin training
"What's the first mission?"

shd_074.title: Strategy
text: Lira's smile is thin and hard. 'You don't fight the corporation head-on. You fight the pieces. The supply chains, the personnel, the infrastructure. You make it expensive. You make it dangerous. You make every Division 9 operative think twice before accepting an assignment that involves hunting people who can walk through the walls of their bedroom while they sleep.'

'We've been running operations for two years. Nothing flashy. Sabotaging scanner equipment. Intercepting extraction teams. Extracting captured Shadowkin from transit routes before they reach the facilities. Building a network of safe houses, informants, sympathizers. Mira is one of ours. Bao is one of ours. There are others you haven't met.'
She pulls up a list on a handheld datapad. Names, locations, status reports. The infrastructure of a resistance movement built from the margins of a city that doesn't know it exists.

'But now we have a bigger problem than Division 9's capture squads. The Resonance Engine changes everything. If they activate it, they won't need to hunt us. The Engine will rip the dimensional barrier open from this side, and everything in the between-space comes pouring through. We'll be collateral damage in our own extinction event.'

choices.text: "How do we stop the Engine?"
"I need training first. I'm not ready."

shd_075.title: Training
text: Lira takes you to the training area. The walls are scarred with phase damage, patches where reality was stretched and released, leaving the concrete warped and discolored. Other Shadowkin practice here, you can see two of them across the cavern, phasing through obstacles with a precision that makes your crude survival phasing look like a child's first steps.

'First lesson,' Lira says. 'The phase isn't something that happens to you. It's something you do. Your body learned it as a fear response, an escape mechanism, but it's a tool. Like a muscle you've only ever used in panic. I'm going to teach you to use it with intention.'
She places her glass hand on the wall. 'Phase your hand into this wall. Just your hand. Keep the rest of you solid.'
You try. Your whole arm phases. Your shoulder starts to go.
'Control,' Lira says sharply. 'The phase follows your focus. Narrow your focus. Your hand. Just your hand. The rest of you is here. Solid. Real. Anchored.'

You try again. And again. And again. Each attempt burns willpower, drains energy, leaves you shaking and sweat-soaked. But by the twentieth attempt, you can feel the difference. The phase responding to direction instead of emotion. A tool instead of a curse.
It's the first time in thirteen years that the phasing has felt like something other than a death sentence.

choices.text: Continue training for days
"I'm ready. What's the mission?"

shd_076.title: Nightmare
text: You sleep. And you dream.

In the dream, you're standing in the between-space. Not passing through it, not phasing, but standing in it as if it were a place, a room with no walls, a landscape of absolute darkness that extends in every direction forever.
And you're not alone.

Something stands in front of you. Not a shape, not a figure. An absence. A hole in the darkness that's somehow darker than the dark around it, as if the thing is made of a more concentrated version of nothing. It has no face, no features, no body. But it has presence. A gravity. An attention that falls on you like a spotlight made of ice.
It speaks. Not in words. In feeling. In the raw, preverbal language of dimensional resonance. It says: DOOR. It says: SOON. It says: READY.

You wake screaming, your entire body phased, your bedroll passing through you, your hands sunk into the concrete floor. It takes you five minutes to solidify completely. Five minutes of fighting the phase with everything you have, pulling yourself back from the between-space centimeter by centimeter.
Something is coming. Something that knows your name.

choices.text: You need answers. Find the Fold.
Go to Mira. She'll know what this means.

shd_077.title: Alone No More
text: Mira sets down her soldering iron and looks at you with the expression of someone about to say something they've been holding back. 'You're not alone. You just think you are because alone is all you've ever been. But there are others. More than you know.'

She pulls up her phase-event map. Dots of light scattered across the Lower District, each one representing a detected phase event, a moment where a Shadowkin crossed between dimensions. 'Fourteen unique signatures in the last month. You're one of them. Fourteen Shadowkin, living in this district, and none of you know about each other because you're all too afraid to reach out.'

'There's a group forming. The Fold. Their leader, Lira, she's building something in the deep tunnels. Something real. If you went to her, if you brought her your skills and your knowledge of the tunnel network, you'd have allies. You'd have a purpose. You'd have something worth more than survival.'
She hands you the repaired stabilizer. 'Take this. Go find her. And when you get there, tell her Mira sent you. She'll trust you faster.'

choices.text: Head into the deep tunnels to find the Fold
"Come with me, Mira."

shd_078.title: The Foundry Plan
text: The Foundry district fills the air with heat and noise even at street level. Below, in the industrial sublevels, the temperature climbs to levels that would cook unprotected skin. The factories run around the clock, automated production lines stamping out components for the megacity's endless appetite for technology, weapons, and replacement parts for the human body.

Somewhere beneath this industrial labyrinth is the original lab. Project Threshold's birthplace. The place where your kind was accidentally created by scientists playing with forces they didn't understand.

Getting in won't be easy, even for someone who can walk through walls. The industrial infrastructure is dense, layers of metal and concrete and active machinery, with lethal hazards on every level. Phase through the wrong wall and you could materialize inside a blast furnace. Miss your exit point and you'd be trapped in solid steel.
But the potential reward, a corporate-grade stabilizer, maybe even data about Project Threshold, is worth the risk.
You approach the Foundry's perimeter, feeling the heat rising through the ground like fever from sick earth.

choices.text: Scout the perimeter for an entry point
Go find backup first. This is too dangerous alone.

shd_079.title: The Lost Girl
text: Mira's hands shake. It's subtle, a tremor in her fingers that she controls by gripping the edge of the workbench. 'The instruments say yes. Her consciousness is still broadcasting on the phase frequency. They record it. They study it. They analyze her screams like data points on a chart.'

'She's been in the between for six years. Six years of existing as pure consciousness in a space that wasn't designed for consciousness. I don't know if what's left of her is still her. I don't know if there's enough of a person remaining to save.'

She looks at you, and for the first time, you see something behind the clinical detachment. Guilt. The kind that doesn't erode with time, just calcifies, becomes part of the architecture of who you are.

'I helped build the equipment that did that to her. I calibrated the instruments. I signed the reports. I told myself it was science, that it was necessary, that the data would help us understand the phenomenon. And then I heard her screaming through the dimensional barrier and I understood that I hadn't been doing science. I'd been doing violence.'
'If there's any way to bring her back, any way at all, I need to find it. I owe her that.'

choices.text: "We'll find a way. I promise."
"Another reason to destroy Division 9."

shd_080.title: Impossible Choice
text: Mira shakes her head. 'You can't stop phasing. It's not voluntary, not entirely. Your body exists in a state of superposition. Suppressing the phase doesn't eliminate it, just delays it, and when the delayed phase finally triggers, it's more powerful, more destabilizing, more dangerous. The stabilizer doesn't stop phasing. It regulates it. Controlled releases of dimensional energy, like a pressure valve.'

'Take the stabilizer away, try to suppress the phase through willpower alone, and eventually you'll have a catastrophic event. A phase so powerful it tears through the local dimensional barrier like a bullet through paper. You'd take everyone in a fifty-meter radius into the between-space with you.'
She lets that sink in. Fifty meters. In the Lower District, that's hundreds of people. Families. Children.

'The only way to stop the acceleration is to deal with the source. The original rift. The Resonance Engine. Whatever Division 9 is building in the Foundry. We have to shut it down, or we have to find a way to seal the barrier permanently.'
'Neither of those options is going to be easy. Or safe. Or anything less than insane.'

choices.text: "Then we go insane. What's the plan?"
"I need to find the Fold first. Build a team."

shd_081.title: Seal the Breach
text: 'Strengthen the barrier,' Mira repeats, turning the idea over like a physical object. 'In theory, it's possible. The original Project Threshold equipment included phase-dampening technology, the inverse of what they were trying to achieve. If that equipment still exists, if it's still functional, it could potentially be repurposed to reinforce the dimensional barrier instead of weakening it.'

'But here's the irony. The only people who could operate that equipment are people with phase abilities. Baseline humans can't interact with dimensional technology. It requires a consciousness that exists in both spaces simultaneously. It requires a Shadowkin.'

'So the same phenomenon that's weakening the barrier is the only thing that can repair it. Your curse is also your cure. And Division 9 knows this. That's why they're building the Resonance Engine. Not to open the barrier. To control who gets to close it, and what they demand in exchange.'

The implications settle into your mind like sediment in water. Division 9 isn't just experimenting. They're positioning themselves as the gatekeepers of dimensional stability. The saviors who hold the key. And the price of salvation will be whatever they decide to charge.

choices.text: "We need to get to that equipment first."
"Find the Fold. We need numbers for this."

shd_082.title: Watching Mira's Back
text: You circle back through the alleys, phasing through walls to stay hidden, keeping an eye on the market strip from the between-space. It's exhausting, maintaining a partial phase while moving, but you need to know Mira got clear.

You see her emerge from the back of her shop, bug-out bag on her back, moving through the market crowd with the calm efficiency of someone who's rehearsed this escape. She doesn't look back. She doesn't hesitate. She walks to the transit hub entrance and disappears into the crowd of commuters.
Good. She's clear.

Then you see the Division 9 team. Four of them, grey coats, scanner equipment, moving through the crowd with the focused intensity of hunting dogs on a scent. They converge on Mira's shop. Find it empty. One of them speaks into a comm unit, fast, urgent.
They know. And they're going to start searching. The tunnels, the transit system, every bolt-hole and safe house in the district. The clock is ticking.
You grip the data chip Mira gave you and head underground.

choices.text: Find the Fold. Deliver the data.
Go to the Foundry. Stop the Engine.

shd_083.title: Organization
text: Harsk stares into the barrel fire. 'Organize? Us? We can't organize a food rotation without three arguments and a fistfight. How are we going to organize against Division 9?'

'By doing what we're already doing, but together,' you say. 'Early warning systems. Watchers at the tunnel entrances. A communication network so when they come for one of us, all of us know about it.'
The old man considers this. Around the fire, other tunnel dwellers have stopped what they're doing to listen. You have an audience. You didn't plan for an audience.

'The shadow-kid has a point,' a woman says from the edge of the group. She's tall, gaunt, with the haunted eyes of someone who's seen too much to be surprised by anything. 'Maya was one of us. She wasn't Shadowkin, wasn't important to anyone except us. They took her because she was convenient. Because nobody would notice.'
'We noticed,' you say. 'And next time, we do more than notice.'
The tunnel community looks at you with something you haven't seen directed at you before. Not fear. Not suspicion. Respect.

choices.text: Help set up the warning network
Ask about the Fold

shd_084.title: Fold Rumors
text: Harsk's expression shifts when you mention the Fold. Not surprise. Recognition. 'I've heard of them. Everyone in the deep tunnels has. A woman named Lira, gathering Shadowkin, building something in the old sections below the reactor shielding. Nobody down here goes that deep, not if they like breathing.'

'But she does. She and her people move through the deep tunnels like they own them. I've seen the symbols. The circle with the line. Some of my people have seen her, talked to her. She offered protection. Sanctuary. Some of them went. They haven't come back, which either means they found something worth staying for or something found them.'

He pokes the barrel fire, sending sparks spiraling upward. 'If you're thinking about going down there, take some advice from an old man who's survived by being a coward: go with your eyes open. People who offer salvation in the dark usually want something in return.'
'Everything good in the Lower District comes with a price. The question is whether you can afford it.'

choices.text: Go find the Fold
Set up the warning network first, then go

shd_085.title: Bao's Contact
text: Bao's contact arrives forty minutes later. A woman in a patched jacket, moving through the rain with the quiet confidence of someone who knows these streets intimately. The tattoos on her arms shift in the lamplight.
Lira.

She enters Bao's storage room and kneels beside Pyx with the practiced gentleness of someone who's done this before. Who's rescued children before. Her glass hand touches the child's forehead, and Pyx's flickering outline steadies, the phase response calming in the presence of another Shadowkin.

'Subject 47,' Lira murmurs, reading the Division 9 restraint tag still attached to Pyx's wrist. 'They're getting bolder. Taking them younger.' She snaps the tag off and crushes it in her glass hand, the transparent fingers closing around it with a strength that belies their fragile appearance.
She looks up at you. 'You did this? Pulled a child out of a Division 9 extraction?'
'Yes.'

Her expression is evaluating, calculating, measuring you against some internal standard. Then she nods. 'We need people like you. Come with me. Both of you. I have a safe place, and the child needs medical attention I can't provide here.'

choices.text: Go with Lira
"First, tell me who you are."

shd_086.title: The Plan
text: Lira gathers the Fold. All of them. Twenty-three Shadowkin standing in the sanctuary's main cavern, their outlines flickering in the lamplight, their faces a spectrum of determination and fear. You stand among them and feel, for the first time, like you belong somewhere.

'Phase one,' Lira says, her voice carrying in the cavern's natural acoustics. 'Intelligence. We need to know what Division 9 has built beneath the Foundry. Layout, security, the Resonance Engine's configuration. We send our best infiltrator.' Her eyes find you. 'Someone who can phase through the heaviest security and come back with what we need.'

'Phase two: allies. We reach out beyond the Shadowkin community. The tunnel dwellers, the market vendors, the sympathizers who've been helping us in secret. We build a coalition. Because what Division 9 is planning doesn't just threaten us. If the dimensional barrier collapses, it threatens everyone.'
'Phase three: assault. We go into the Foundry, we reach the Engine, and we shut it down. Permanently. Whatever it takes.'

She pauses. Looks at each face in turn. 'This is what we were made for. Not by choice, not by design, but by accident and survival. We are the rift's children, and it's our responsibility to close the door that should never have been opened.'
The silence that follows is absolute. Then, one by one, the Shadowkin of the Fold nod their agreement.

choices.text: Volunteer for the infiltration mission
Focus on building the coalition

shd_087.title: Urgency
text: 'We move against Division 9,' Lira agrees. 'But we do it smart. A direct assault on their facilities would be suicide. They have resources we can't match, firepower, personnel, technology designed specifically to contain and neutralize Shadowkin.'

'What we have is the ability to be where they don't expect us. To pass through their defenses like they don't exist. To strike from angles that conventional security can't cover. We are ghosts, and it's time we started haunting them.'
She turns to Thorne, the big non-Shadowkin who serves as the Fold's security chief. 'Prepare the team. Standard infiltration loadout. We move in forty-eight hours.'

Thorne nods and begins organizing. Around you, the sanctuary transforms from a refuge into an operations center. Shadowkin move with purpose, checking equipment, reviewing routes, preparing for something that feels less like a mission and more like a reckoning.

You watch them and feel the weight of what's coming settle onto your shoulders. You've spent thirteen years running from what you are. Now you're about to charge directly into the place where what you are was born. The symmetry is poetic. The terror is very, very real.

choices.text: Prepare for the mission
Get some training in before you go

shd_088.title: Days of Training
text: Three days. Lira pushes you for three days straight, and you learn more about the phase in seventy-two hours than you did in thirteen years of stumbling through it alone.

You learn selective phasing, making parts of your body intangible while keeping the rest solid. You learn phase-sight, the ability to see through the dimensional membrane into the between-space without fully entering it. You learn the anchor technique, fixing your consciousness to a physical point in real space so the between-space can't pull you in during extended phases.

And you learn to phase others. To extend your dimensional shift to encompass another person, carrying them through solid matter the way you carried Pyx through the tunnel wall. It's exhausting, draining, and incredibly dangerous, but when it works, it's the closest thing to a miracle you've ever performed.

On the third day, Lira watches you phase through a three-meter wall, turn, and phase back through it in the opposite direction without stopping. When you emerge, solid and barely winded, she smiles. It's the first real smile you've seen from her.
'You're ready,' she says. 'Not perfect. Not safe. But ready.'

choices.text: "What's the mission?"
"I need to know more about the Engine first."

shd_089.title: Mira Joins
text: Mira looks at you for a long moment. Then she starts packing. Not the bug-out bag, the quick-escape kit she keeps ready for emergencies. She packs methodically, deliberately, selecting tools and components from her workbench with the precision of a surgeon choosing instruments.

'I've been hiding in this shop for four years,' she says, filling a case with stabilizer components. 'Fixing your equipment, gathering data, telling myself I was helping from the sidelines. But sidelines are just a comfortable word for cowardice.'

She closes the case and picks up her taser. 'If the Fold is building something real, they'll need an engineer. Someone who understands phase technology from the inside. Someone who knows what Division 9 has and how to counter it.'

'Besides,' she adds, her mouth twisting in a humorless smile, 'this shop has been watched for days. It's only a matter of time before they come through the door. Better to leave on my terms than theirs.'
You lead her to the maintenance hatch. Into the tunnels. Into the dark. Toward something that might be salvation or might be one more catastrophe in a life full of them.
But at least you're not walking alone.

choices.text: Take Mira to the Fold's sanctuary
Go directly to the Foundry with Mira

shd_090.title: The Foundry Infiltration
text: The Foundry's industrial sublevel is a hellscape of heat and noise. Automated forges pound like iron heartbeats. Conveyor systems carry molten components through channels cut into the floor. The air shimmers with thermal distortion, turning everything beyond arm's length into a haze of mirages.

You descend alone. Lira wanted to send a team, but teams can't do what you can do. Teams need doors and corridors and access codes. You need nothing but the phase and the willpower to hold it steady in conditions that would shatter most Shadowkin's concentration.

You phase through the factory floor and into the levels below. The industrial noise fades. The heat diminishes. The architecture changes, from modern manufacturing to something older, heavier, built to contain rather than produce. You're entering the old lab. Project Threshold's birthplace.

The corridors are dark, but your phase-sight illuminates them in the spectral glow of residual dimensional energy. This place is saturated with it. Every wall, every surface, every molecule of air vibrates with the echo of the experiment that created your kind.
And ahead, in the deepest chamber, something enormous hums with a frequency that resonates in your bones.

choices.text: Move toward the source of the humming
Search the labs for equipment first

shd_091.title: Building the Network
text: You spend two days building the coalition. Moving through the tunnel communities, the market stalls, the back rooms of noodle bars and repair shops. Talking to people who've been living in the margins, explaining what Division 9 is doing, what the Resonance Engine means, what happens if nobody acts.

Most listen. Some refuse. Some are already helping, in small ways, a hidden message here, a diverted patrol there. The infrastructure of resistance exists in the Lower District the way root systems exist under a forest floor, invisible until you know where to look.

By the end of the second day, you have watchers at twelve tunnel entrances, a communication relay using repurposed comm units, and a network of safe houses that can shelter Shadowkin in emergencies. It's not an army. It's barely a neighborhood watch. But it's real, and it's connected, and when Division 9 moves, they'll know about it.

Harsk shakes your hand when it's done. His grip is rough and honest. 'For what it's worth,' he says, 'the old man is impressed. Don't let it go to your head. Impressed and useful aren't the same thing.'
But you can hear the respect underneath the gruff. And respect, in the Lower District, is the foundation of everything.

choices.text: Now it's time for the Foundry mission
Report back to the Fold

shd_092.title: The Resonance Engine
text: The chamber is vast. A natural cavern widened by engineering into a space that could hold a starship. And in its center, the Resonance Engine.

It's not what you expected. You expected machinery, industrial, recognizable, something made of metal and wire and purpose. What you see is something else entirely. A column of captured nothing, a vertical shaft of between-space held in place by a ring of phase-dampening emitters, each one pulsing with the frequency that makes your stabilizer scream.

The column of between-space is twenty meters tall and three meters wide. Inside it, the hungry dark writhes and coils, and you can see shapes moving in its depths. The shapes you saw in your dreams. The presences. The things that live in the gap between dimensions.

They're pushing against the column's boundary. Testing it. Pressing against the phase-dampening field with a patient, relentless pressure that has been building for years. And the field is failing. You can see the cracks, hairline fractures in the dimensional barrier where the between-space bleeds through into reality, tiny tendrils of absolute darkness reaching into the cavern like fingers probing a wound.
Division 9 hasn't built a weapon. They've built a door. And something on the other side is about to push it open.

choices.text: Try to shut down the emitters
Get this intel back to the Fold immediately

shd_093.title: The Old Labs
text: The labs are preserved like a crime scene nobody processed. Equipment still on the benches, data terminals still powered by some deep infrastructure connection that's been running for twenty-three years. Everything covered in a fine layer of dust except the paths worn by recent foot traffic. Division 9 has been here. Recently.

You find a stabilizer. Not a homemade one, not a stopgap. A real, corporate-grade phase stabilizer, sleek and powerful, designed by people who understood the technology at a fundamental level. It sits in a storage case alongside three others, all fully charged, all functional. Equipment prepared for test subjects who never arrived.

You take one. The moment you put it on, the difference is staggering. The phase response goes quiet, truly quiet, in a way your homemade stabilizer never achieved. The tingling in your fingers stops. The blurring at your edges firms up. For the first time in years, you feel completely, unambiguously solid.

On the data terminal, you find files. Project Threshold research logs. Subject records. Schematics for the Resonance Engine. The complete blueprint of Division 9's twenty-three-year operation to crack open the dimensional barrier.
You download everything onto a chip.

choices.text: Now find the Resonance Engine
Get this data back to the Fold

shd_094.title: Confrontation
text: You move toward the nearest emitter, phasing through the security barriers that ring the Engine chamber. Your new stabilizer handles the proximity to the between-space column without strain, its corporate-grade dampening field holding steady where your old one would have been screaming.
Then the lights come on. All of them. The chamber floods with white light, and you see that you're not alone.

Division 9. A full tactical squad, positioned around the chamber's perimeter, weapons trained on you. And in the center, standing beside the Engine's control console, a woman in a grey coat. Dr. Elara Voss, the name on the Project Threshold research logs. The scientist who started all of this, twenty-three years ago.

'Subject 23,' she says, consulting a datapad. 'We've been expecting you. Or rather, we've been expecting one of you. The phase signature you've been broadcasting since you entered the Foundry has been quite informative.'
She smiles. It doesn't reach her eyes. 'Welcome home.'

choices.text: Phase out immediately
"You know what's on the other side of that column. You know what you're about to unleash."

shd_095.title: Escape with Intel
text: You've seen enough. The Engine, the labs, the scope of Division 9's operation, it's all worse than anyone imagined. The Fold needs this information. Lira needs to know what they're up against.

You turn to leave and the alarm triggers. A phase-detection grid, built into the floor, invisible until activated. Red lights spin. Klaxons blare. The sound is a physical assault in the confined space of the old labs.

You run. Phase through walls, solid through corridors, phase again. The Foundry's industrial levels flash past in a blur of heat and steel. Behind you, boots on metal, the whine of neural stunners charging, voices shouting orders. They're fast, but you're faster. You know how to disappear.

You burst out of the Foundry's perimeter into the cool night air of the Lower District. Rain hits your face. The city spreads around you, dark and loud and alive. You keep running, into the alleys, toward the nearest tunnel entrance, leaving the Foundry and its terrible secret behind you.
The data chip burns against your chest like a coal. The information on it will change everything the Fold thinks they know about Division 9, about the Engine, about what's coming.

choices.text: Get back to the Fold's sanctuary
Warn Mira first

shd_096.title: Breaking Free
text: You phase. Full shift, instant, the fastest transition you've ever managed. The neural stunner bolts pass through you like light through glass. Dr. Voss's eyes widen, the first genuine emotion you've seen on her face, surprise bordering on alarm.

In the phase state, you can see the Engine differently. The column of between-space isn't just a captured phenomenon. It's a wound. A place where reality has been torn open and held apart by force, the dimensional equivalent of holding a wound open so it can't heal. The things inside the column press against its boundaries with renewed urgency, sensing you, recognizing you, one of the rift's children standing at the edge of the rift itself.

You sprint through the chamber in phase, past the tactical squad, through the walls, through the security barriers, through the Foundry's industrial heart. Behind you, Dr. Voss shouts orders. Around you, phase-dampening fields snap to life, trying to force you solid, trying to trap you in the between-space.
But Lira trained you. You know how to anchor. You know how to hold your connection to real space even when everything in the between is trying to keep you there.
You burst out of the Foundry and into the night, solid, gasping, running. You have what you came for. You know what the Engine is. Now you need to tell the world.

choices.text: Race to the Fold
Find Mira. She needs to know.

shd_097.title: Face to Face
text: Dr. Voss tilts her head. 'Of course I know. I've known since the first experiment. The between-space isn't empty. It contains entities that operate on principles our physics can't describe. They're drawn to phase events the way sharks are drawn to blood in water.'
'And you're opening the door for them,' you say.

'I'm opening a controlled portal,' she corrects. 'The difference is engineering. The entities can be contained, studied, potentially harnessed. The energy potential of the between-space is limitless. Meridian's shareholders understand that the risk-reward calculus favors action.'

'Your shareholders have never been to the between,' you say. 'I have. Whatever's in there isn't going to be contained. It isn't going to be harnessed. It's been pushing against the barrier for twenty-three years, and it's hungry, and it's patient, and the moment you open that door wide enough for it to get through, you're not going to close it again.'
For a moment, something flickers across Dr. Voss's face. Doubt. The kind of doubt that lives underneath certainty, the quiet voice that whispers what if you're wrong in the middle of the night.
Then it's gone, replaced by the cold composure of someone who's invested too much to turn back. 'Take them,' she says to the tactical squad.

choices.text: Phase out. Now.
"Your experiment is killing children. Subject 47. Pyx. How many more?"

shd_098.title: Return to the Fold
text: You descend into the tunnels at a dead sprint, following the phase symbols through the darkness, navigating by instinct and muscle memory through passages that no solid person could follow. The sanctuary opens before you, and you burst in gasping, rain-soaked, carrying the weight of everything you've learned.

Lira meets you at the entrance. One look at your face tells her enough. She gathers the Fold in the main cavern, and you tell them everything. The Engine. The column of captured between-space. The entities pressing against the barrier. Dr. Voss and her twenty-three-year plan to crack open reality like an egg.

The sanctuary goes silent when you finish. Twenty-three Shadowkin, each one a living consequence of Project Threshold, standing in the dark and absorbing the news that the experiment that created them is about to be completed at any cost.
Lira breaks the silence. 'Now we know. And now we fight. Not tomorrow. Not next week. Now.'

She turns to you. 'You've proven yourself tonight. Whatever comes next, whatever we face in the Foundry, you'll be at the front. You're one of us. The rift's children, standing between the world and what's trying to break through.'
You look around at the faces in the lamplight. Your people. Your kind. Your family, chosen by accident and forged by survival.
Whatever comes next, you face it together.

choices.text: Stand with the Fold and prepare for what's coming

shd_099.title: Warning Mira
text: You find Mira at one of her backup locations, a storage unit in the transit hub that she's converted into a miniature workshop. She opens the door with her taser drawn, relaxing only slightly when she sees your face.
'You look like you've seen something,' she says.

You tell her about the Engine. The column of between-space. The entities. Dr. Voss. You watch her face as she processes the information, the engineer's mind working through the implications, the scale, the catastrophic potential.

'A captured column of between-space,' she repeats. 'The phase-dampening emitters are creating a containment field, holding the portal open while preventing the entities from fully manifesting. But if the field degrades, or if they increase the aperture deliberately...'
'Everything in the between-space comes through,' you finish.

Mira sits down heavily. 'I helped design the containment protocols. Twenty years ago, before I understood what we were containing. The protocols were meant for small-scale phase events. What you're describing is... they've scaled it beyond anything I thought possible. Beyond anything that should be possible.'
She looks up. 'Take me to the Fold. I need to see Lira's data, and she needs to see mine. Together, we might be able to find a way to shut the Engine down without catastrophic blowback. Maybe.'

choices.text: Take Mira to the Fold's sanctuary

shd_100.title: Between Worlds
text: Dawn doesn't reach the sanctuary. There is no dawn here, only the steady glow of the luminescent mural and the hum of the stabilizers that keep twenty-four Shadowkin anchored to a reality that has never fully accepted them.

You stand before the mural, looking at the painted figures emerging from shadow. Tomorrow, the Fold moves against Division 9. Tomorrow, you walk back into the Foundry and face the Engine and the entities and Dr. Voss and twenty-three years of corporate indifference to the lives your kind has lost. Tomorrow, everything changes.
But tonight, you stand in the dark with your people and you feel something you haven't felt since your mother held your hand in a transit hub twenty years ago.
You feel whole.

Not solid. Whole. There's a difference. Solidity is a state of matter. Wholeness is a state of soul. You exist between dimensions, between real and not-real, between here and the hungry dark of the between-space. You've spent your life treating that as a curse, a deficiency, a mistake written into your DNA by a corporation that didn't care what it created.

But standing here, surrounded by others who share your impossible existence, you understand for the first time that the space between worlds isn't empty. It's where you live. It's where you belong. And it's worth fighting for.
The shadows dance in the lamplight. For once, you don't flinch.

Tomorrow, you go to war. Tonight, you rest. And when the phase comes in your sleep, reaching for you with its cold, familiar hands, you don't fight it. You let it carry you to the edge of the between, and you look into the dark, and you whisper:
I'm not afraid of you anymore.

choices.text: Face the dawn between worlds
